airfields.name: Pitomnik
airfields.notes: Main supply airfield inside the Stalingrad pocket; the airlift's lifeline. Captured by the Soviets on 16 January 1943, collapsing the resupply effort.

airfields.name: Gumrak
airfields.notes: Secondary airfield inside the Stalingrad pocket and site of 6. Armee HQ. The last functioning strip after Pitomnik fell; abandoned 22 January 1943.

airfields.name: Tatsinskaya
airfields.notes: Principal Ju 52 transport base for the Stalingrad airlift. Overrun by 24th Tank Corps in the Tatsinskaya Raid, 24 December 1942, with heavy aircraft losses.

airfields.name: Morozovskaya
airfields.notes: He 111 bomber/transport base supporting the airlift, paired with Tatsinskaya. Evacuated under Soviet pressure in early January 1943.

airfields.name: Oblivskaya
airfields.notes: Forward fighter and transport strip on the Chir, covering the approaches to the Don airlift fields.

airfields.name: Karpovka
airfields.notes: Strip on the western edge of the pocket used in the early encirclement before the line contracted.

airfields.name: Leninsk
airfields.notes: Soviet field east of the Volga, in the 8th Air Army's basing belt safely beyond German artillery range.

airfields.name: Solodniki
airfields.notes: Soviet airfield on the east bank of the Volga south of Stalingrad, used by fighter and Shturmovik regiments.

airfields.name: Srednyaya Akhtuba
airfields.notes: Forward Soviet field just east of the Volga opposite the city, within minutes' flying time of the Stalingrad battle zone.
